name: Qorvo, Inc.
business: Qorvo is a global leader in the development and commercialization of technologies and products for wireless, wired and power markets. It operates in three segments: High Performance Analog (HPA) supplying RF, analog mixed signal and power management solutions; Connectivity and Sensors Group (CSG) supplying connectivity solutions including UWB, Wi-Fi, BLE, and IoT; and Advanced Cellular Group (ACG) supplying advanced cellular solutions for smartphones, wearables, laptops, tablets and other devices. The company serves six primary end markets: automotive, consumer, defense and aerospace, industrial and enterprise, infrastructure, and mobile.

exposures.sentence: Focus areas include the Golden Dome defense system; upgrades to legacy radar systems; the shift to new, higher frequency bands; and the trend of one to many, in which a greater number of smaller, more highly integrated and scalable networked platforms (e.g., unmanned vehicles such as aerial drones and ground robots) are required to address the rapidly evolving technology landscape being deployed across military applications.

HPA also supplies products for satellites and terminals to the leading companies in low Earth orbit ("LEO") satellite communication ("SATCOM") networks where investment is increasing globally to provide enhanced and more ubiquitous connectivity for commercial and other applications.
In enterprise data centers, HPA's PMIC solutions address the artificial intelligence ("AI")-driven demand for highly efficient enterprise solid-state drive ("SSD") power.

We also provide differentiated solutions for motor drive and control across industrial and enterprise applications including factory motor drives and controls, large power tools, factory transport systems, robotics, industrial drones, pumps and fans.
In cellular base stations, the migration to 5G increases capacity, expands coverage and lowers the cost per bit of data.
In Wi-Fi, new standards and architectures such as 802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 6E) and 802.11be (Wi-Fi 7), are enhancing performance, increasing range and capacity and enabling new use cases.

In automotive markets, new use cases including vehicle-to-everything ("V2X") communications, advanced connectivity services and secure car access are supporting the migration to more connected, more intelligent vehicles.

Qorvo s multi-protocol SoCs enable multiple radios to connect concurrently. The coexistence of multiple low-power wireless protocols in a SoC reduces form factor, extends battery life and helps advance the proliferation of IoT devices.
Qorvo is a global leader in the development and commercialization of technologies and products for wireless, wired and power markets.

catalysts.event: Merger completion with Skyworks expected early calendar year 2027
catalysts.sentence: We currently anticipate the Mergers will be completed early in calendar year 2027.
